business.headline: JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

Fake bosses, real losses: Sebi warns of scammers posing as CEOs in rising boss scamSebi has warned companies about a rising 'boss scam' in which fraudsters impersonate senior executives to trick employees into transferring funds, and urged firms to strengthen their security mechanisms and enhance vigilance.

F1 could return to India in 2028 as government races to fix old tax hurdlesThe government has begun talks with stakeholders to revive Formula One in India by 2028. The push centres on fixing old tax and regulatory hurdles that derailed the Indian Grand Prix.

VFS Global continues to invest in AI to streamline visa application processingSpeaking at India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, Yummi Talwar, COO, South Asia, VFS Global, said visa demand has largely returned to normal after the post-pandemic surge.

Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

Tata, SP Group in fresh talks on Tata Sons stake swapTata Sons and the SP Group are exploring a share swap to monetise part of the latter's stake. The talks matter because they could reduce SP Group debt, though valuation and structure remain unresolved.

entertainment.headline: The India Story trailer: Kajal Aggarwal, Shreyas Talpade battle pesticide crisis The trailer of The India Story: Slow Poison in Progress is out. Starring Shreyas Talpade and Kajal Aggarwal, the film follows a father and a lawyer confronting the fallout of pesticide farming.

Why Haiwaan could be Saif Ali Khan's boldest reinvention yet Haiwaan reunites actor Saif Ali Khan with director Priyadarshan as he plays a visually challenged protagonist. The role could recast the mainstream Hindi action hero through vulnerability, instinct and empathy.

Why cry? Annu Kapoor backs Satluj takedown, urges makers to seek legal route Actor Annu Kapoor has backed the government's stand on Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj and said the makers should move the Supreme Court. His remarks come as the film's removal from ZEE5 has intensified debate over censorship and creative freedom.

Varanasi: Priyanka Chopra's fierce look as Mandakini revealed on birthday SS Rajamouli unveiled Priyanka Chopra's first look as Mandakini from Varanasi on her birthday. The reveal underlines her central role in the globe-spanning epic.

Why Cillian Murphy felt relief, not regret, over missing Nolan's The Odyssey Actor Cillian Murphy said he felt 'relief' about not joining Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He called the absence a gift because it let him watch the film purely as an audience member.

Lawrence Bishnoi's brother threatens Aamir Khan over 'love jihaad' remarks Gangster Lawrence Bishnoi's brother Arzoo Bishnoi has issued a warning to actor Aamir Khan over his remarks and views on 'love jihad'. In a purported audio clip, Arzoo claimed that the actor's actions were against the culture and warned of consequences.

What is IMAX, how does it work? The maths you didn't know behind Nolan's The Odyssey Christopher Nolan's India visit for The Odyssey has revived questions about what IMAX really means in the country. The debate centres on the gap between India's commercial screens and the true IMAX format his films are designed for.

Greatest pleasure of my career: Tom Holland thanks Nolan amid The Odyssey release Actor Tom Holland marked the release of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey with a gratitude note on social media. His message highlighted the film as a creatively fulfilling experience as it opened strongly in India.

The Odyssey box office Day 1: Nolan film joins India's biggest Hollywood openers Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey opened strongly in India, led by the English version and IMAX shows. The start underlines strong pre-release buzz and audience preference for premium large-format screenings.

Jennifer Winget's viral bridal gown clip fuels wedding rumours. Watch Wedding rumours surrounding Jennifer Winget have intensified after a behind-the-scenes video of her bridal gown fittings went viral on social media. The actor has not responded to reports claiming she married Singapore-based businessman William Ishmael in a private UK ceremony.

Don't book The Odyssey tickets before reading this IMAX movie format guide Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey was filmed entirely with IMAX cameras, but audiences can choose from several theatrical formats, including IMAX 70mm, Dolby Cinema and PLF. The film's official website explains the differences between each format to help viewers pick their preferred big-screen experience.

Assam govt approaches Aditya Dhar to make biopic on army general Lachit Borphukan Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma has reached out to Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar for a biopic on Lachit Borphukan. Discussions on the project are currently underway.

Sonam Wangchuk thanks Sonakshi Sinha, Omi Vaidya for support amid hunger strike A message from Sonam Wangchuk's office appeared on Sonakshi Sinha and Omi Vaidya's posts after they shared messages of solidarity amid his hunger strike.

Rasika Dugal hopes Mirzapur's loyal OTT fans turn up in theatres Actor Rasika Dugal said returning to Mirzapur for its film version felt exciting and calmer. She said the move from streaming to cinemas and revisiting Beena Tripathi brought both curiosity and nerves.

Actor Murali Sharma on why Telugu cinema is moving beyond masala entertainers Veteran actor Murali Sharma says Telugu cinema has evolved beyond masala entertainers, with even top stars embracing socially relevant stories. Citing Nandamuri Balakrishna's Bhagavanth Kesari, he says such films have the power to influence audiences on a massive scale.

Oscar-winning actor Brenda Fricker, known for Home Alone 2, dies at 81 Oscar-winning Irish actor Brenda Fricker, best known for My Left Foot, Home Alone 2 and Casualty, died at the age of 81. She made history in 1990 as the first Irish female actor to win an Academy Award.

A new low: Internet reacts as Aamir says Wangchuk didn't inspire 3 Idiots' Wangdu Actor Aamir Khan's denial that Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was inspired by Sonam Wangchuk has sparked a heated debate online. Social media users pointed to Wangchuk's old interviews and questioned the actor's claim that the filmmakers did not know the activist while making the film.

He wanted Akshay out of film: OMG 2 makers shred Paresh Rawal over story-theft claim The controversy around the story credit for OMG 2 intensified as producer Ashwin Varde refuted Paresh Rawal's claims. Varde accused Rawal of attempting to create a version of OMG without Akshay Kumar.

CM Vijay's birthday cake becomes a hit in Chennai, 400 sold in 2 weeks: Baker A cake from Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's 52nd birthday celebration has become a hit in Chennai. The baker says she has sold 300 to 400 mango vanilla cakes, now popularly known as the 'CM Cake', after a birthday photo shared by Trisha drew attention to it.

general news.headline: India gets a new rocket: Skyroot's Vikram-1 performs textbook launch on first tryStanding nearly seven storeys tall, Vikram-1 is an all-carbon composite, multi-stage launch vehicle capable of placing payloads weighing up to 350 kilograms into Low Earth Orbit.

Skyroot founders call PM Modi after Vikram-1 launch. Here's what he saidPM Modi, during a telephonic conversation with Skyroot Aerospace's founders, said the successful launch of Vikram-1 had vindicated the government's decision to open India's space sector to private players, while adding that he was especially heartened to see such a young team accomplish the historic feat.

Inside Delhi Police's 3-layer dawn op to remove Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar MantarSonam Wangchuk was removed from the Jantar Mantar protest site in a pre-dawn Delhi Police operation and taken to Safdarjung Hospital.

Sharad Pawar faction-NCP merger before NDA entry: Inside BJP's big planSources said the BJP wants Sharad Pawar's NCP faction to reunite with Sunetra Pawar's camp before joining the NDA. The talks have gained weight after the NCP(SP) signalled a softer stand on the proposed delimitation bill.

Sonam Wangchuk's father fasted for Ladakh in 1984. Then Indira Gandhi came to LehSonam Wangchuk's hunger strike has brought Ladakh's demands back into the national spotlight. But decades earlier, his father Sonam Wangyal fasted for the region's Scheduled Tribe demand. Indira Gandhi visited Leh at the time, and in 1989, a constitutional order formally recognised eight Ladakhi tribal communities as STs.

No treatment underway, hospital not giving reports: Wangchuk's wife's big claimGitanjali Angmo also disputed Delhi Police's claim that her husband was removed from the site of his hunger strike at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar in accordance with Delhi High Court's orders. She also asserted that there is no need for any further intervention by the Centre.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

Let us pose for a pic: Tarun Tejpal cites Robert De Niro episode in sex assault caseThe lawyer of Tehelka founder Tarun Tejpal told the Bombay High Court that the complainant had a 'propensity to lie' and her testimony was inconsistent. The woman has alleged that Tejpal sexually assaulted her in the elevator of a five-star resort in Goa in 2013.

51 in maths, IIT, ISRO and rockets: The story of Pawan Kumar ChandanaToday is a big day for India's space ambitions. The launch of Vikram-I this morning was successful. Now, the man behind the launch, an IITian, a former ISRO scientist, and an ambitious entrepreneur who gave India's space journey a new direction, has emerged from the ground to become a sensation and an inspiration for many youngsters. It is a story you must know.

60 seconds of horror in Mumbai lift as muzzled dog lunges at three girlsCCTV footage showed a pet dog lunging at three girls inside a Malad West high-rise lift. The viral clip has renewed concern over handling dogs in shared residential spaces.

Abhishek Banerjee's 'illegally built' MP office demolished, heavy force deployedAuthorities began demolishing Trinamool Congress MP Abhishek Banerjee's office in South 24 Parganas after alleging it was an illegally constructed building, with heavy police deployment and security at the site.

How Skyroot's Vikram-1 success is a generational leap for Indian space programSkyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 from Sriharikota in its maiden orbital-class mission. The mission highlighted how young private teams are extending Isro's legacy with a new public-facing approach.

The other Lionel: How Scaloni made Argentina great againLionel Messi's Argentina are in the FIFA World Cup final once again. But behind this golden era stands the other Lionel - Lionel Scaloni, the accidental manager who rebuilt Argentina, brought Messi back from the brink, and returned the glory days to the Albiceleste.

Sonam Wangchuk's weak, needs constant monitoring: Hospital on activist's healthFollowing the climate activist's removal from the Jantar Mantar protest site, Safdarjung Hospital said that he is weak due to fasting and dehydration but was stable as his health was under continuous watch.

Vikram-1 in space: A day inside India's first private rocket factoryVikram-I, named after Vikram Sarabhai, has launched into space. The moment shifts attention to India's emerging private rocket ecosystem.

Lawrence Bishnoi's brother threatens Aamir Khan over 'love jihaad' remarksGangster Lawrence Bishnoi's brother Arzoo Bishnoi has issued a warning to actor Aamir Khan over his remarks and views on 'love jihad'. In a purported audio clip, Arzoo claimed that the actor's actions were against the culture and warned of consequences.

We're home to Tagore and Ruskin Bond. So why is literary tourism missing in India?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Just as Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara inspired Indians to travel to Spain, and Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani made trekking with friends a bucket-list experience, books, too, leave us with places we long to visit.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Live: Woman throws ink on CJP's Abhijit Dipke at Jantar Mantar protest siteJantar Mantar Protest Live: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar for the past 20 days, was shifted to Safdarjung Hospital by the Delhi Police on Saturday morning after his health reportedly deteriorated. According to sources, Wangchuk has been admitted to the emergency ward. He is conscious and his vital parameters are stable. In a statement, the Delhi Police said Wangchuk was moved to the hospital for "essential medical care" following expert medical advice and in compliance with the directions of the Delhi High Court. Following Wangchuk's hospitalisation, the Delhi Police appealed to protesters gathered at Jantar Mantar to vacate the protest site peacefully at the earliest. A heavy deployment of Delhi Police personnel was seen at Jantar Mantar after Wangchuk was taken to the hospital. Stay tuned with indiatoday.in for all live updates.

White sheets, human chain: How police removed Sonam Wangchuk from protest siteA coordinated Delhi Police operation at Jantar Mantar saw plainclothes and uniformed personnel form a human chain, surround Sonam Wangchuk with white bedsheets and remove him from his protest site within minutes, with organisers alleging that the activist was forcibly dragged away.

What is IMAX, how does it work? The maths you didn't know behind Nolan's The OdysseyChristopher Nolan's India visit for The Odyssey has revived questions about what IMAX really means in the country. The debate centres on the gap between India's commercial screens and the true IMAX format his films are designed for.

Who is the pro-Taliban maulana challenging Asim Munir: Drop uniform, join politicsPakistan's powerful military is no stranger to criticism, but few senior leaders have challenged its supremacy as directly as Maulana Fazlur Rehman. The veteran Islamist politician, known for his pro-Taliban views and advocacy of Sharia law, is now openly daring Asim Munir to trade his uniform for the ballot box. Here's all you need to know about the maulana challenging the Field Marshal.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

Gurgaon man quits Rs 25,000 supervisor job, reveals how much he earns driving autoA ride with an auto driver led Zypp India CEO Akash Gupta to discuss the driver's income and work journey. The exchange drew attention online to gig work, earnings flexibility and his effort to educate three children.

sports.headline: Djokovic asks Messi about handling pressure. Argentina star's response is gold At Fanatics Fest in Manhattan, Novak Djokovic asked Lionel Messi how he handles pressure. The answer from the Argentina superstar won hearts and showed how Messi remained true to himself throughout his footballing journey.

Not Jannik Sinner, Carlos Alcaraz: Novak Djokovic names his biggest rival on court Novak Djokovic said on CBS Mornings that his biggest rival has always been himself. His remarks also highlighted how ageing, recovery and crowd support are shaping this stage of his career.

Look at Messi: Ex-cricketer explains why India need Ro-Ko at World Cup 2027 Mohammed Kaif said India should take Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma to the 2027 ODI World Cup, using Lionel Messi as his example. He argued that experience will matter in South African conditions even as questions persist over Rohit's form and the pair's long-term place in the squad.

Smriti Mandhana turns 30, Jemimah celebrates their bond in emotional birthday note Smriti Mandhana celebrated her 30th birthday with an emotional message from Jemimah Rodrigues, who reflected on the deep bond they have built over the years.

ODI World Cup: Associate captains fear ICC is closing doors on emerging teams The ICC's revamped format for the 2027 ODI World Cup has drawn sharp criticism from Associate captains Scott Edwards, Gerhard Erasmus, and Richie Berrington, who believe it makes qualification even tougher for emerging nations.

France vs England, 3rd place preview: The heavyweight clash no one wanted A clash worthy of a World Cup final, but with only bronze on the line. France and England meet in Miami as Didier Deschamps says goodbye, Thomas Tuchel looks for answers and Kylian Mbappe's availability remains in the spotlight.

Kohli leads with touching tribute as cricketing world mourns Sir Garfield Sobers Virat Kohli paid an emotional tribute to Sir Garfield Sobers after the West Indies legend passed away at the age of 89 on Thursday, July 17.

Messi reacts to viral bathtub pic with baby Lamine Yamal. Here's what he said Lionel Messi told Tom Brady the story of his iconic bathtub photoshoot with Lamine Yamal ahead of facing the Spanish prodigy in the World Cup 2026 final. Messi met baby Yamal almost 20 years ago during a photoshoot for UNICEF.

Chocolate medal: France, England have no interest in playing 3rd place tie France and England go into the third-place play-off admitting neither side wants it. Didier Deschamps and Thomas Tuchel made their feelings clear that it is just business for them at the moment, and they would rather play the final.

Japan Open 2026: Fiery PV Sindhu storms into first final in 2 years A fiery PV Sindhu produced one of her best performances in recent times as she beat arch-nemesis Chen Yufei to storm into the final of the Japan Open 2026. This ended a two-year wait for Sindhu, who last played a final in 2024.

Donald Trump trolls Tuchel over Harry Kane call in England's semi-final loss Donald Trump mocked Thomas Tuchel's decision to drop Harry Kane into a defensive role during England's semi-final defeat to Argentina. Tuchel came under heavy criticism for his tactics during England's loss in the semi-final.

Spain's Aymeric Laporte feels referees should stop Argentina's physical approachSpain defender Aymeric Laporte has urged referees to keep Argentina's physical approach under control in the FIFA World Cup final. Speaking to Marca, Laporte said officials must ensure the match remains about football and not reckless challenges.

The greatest chapter of my career: Deschamps reflects before France farewellDidier Deschamps called the French national team the "greatest thing" of his professional career as he reflected on his 25-year journey with Les Bleus ahead of his final match in charge against England.

The other Lionel: How Scaloni made Argentina great againLionel Messi's Argentina are in the FIFA World Cup final once again. But behind this golden era stands the other Lionel - Lionel Scaloni, the accidental manager who rebuilt Argentina, brought Messi back from the brink, and returned the glory days to the Albiceleste.

Bengal teen shooter goes missing, found after 48 hours, blames extreme stressFifteen-year-old national rifle shooter Damayanti Sen who went missing from Howrah under mysterious circumstances, has been found after 48 hours. The 15-year-old shooter blamed severe stress as the reason behind her fleeing from home.

Argentina will struggle to make a messy game vs Spain in World Cup finalIndia Today's Consulting Editor Rajdeep Sardesai joins football experts Karim Bencherifa, Ranjeet Bajaj, Siddhant Aney and host Nikhil Naz for a special FIFA World Cup final preview. Can Lionel Messi inspire Argentina to glory, or will Spain's young stars complete their fairytale run? The panel discusses it all in this roundtable session.

Lord's ODI will not be Rohit Sharma's last: BCCI secretary Devajit SaikiaBCCI secretary Devajit Saikia has dismissed speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future, confirming that the former India captain will not retire after the third ODI against England at Lord's. The clarification comes after reports claimed that the Indian team management was planning to move on from the veteran opener ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Chennai Grand Masters: World champion Gukesh stunned by Firouzja in Round 2Alireza Firouzja outlasted world champion D Gukesh in 69 moves at the Chennai Grand Masters. The win gave the French Grandmaster sole lead after two rounds and underlined the ruthlessness of this field.

When Garry Sobers waited in the stands to meet Rohit Sharma and Virat KohliSir Garfield Sobers' greatness was defined not just by his unmatched records but also by his humility. During India's 2023 tour of the West Indies, the cricket legend quietly walked up to Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill after practice, offering a lasting reminder of the man behind the game's greatest all-round legacy.

Sir Garfield Sobers, one of cricket's greatest all-rounders, dies at 89Sir Garfield Sobers, widely regarded as cricket's greatest all-rounder, has died at the age of 89. The West Indies legend leaves behind an unmatched legacy, having redefined excellence with bat, ball and in the field across a glittering international career.

Don't make Rohit feel unwanted: Ashwin calls for lessons from his retirementRavichandran Ashwin is not happy with how things are being handled in the Indian team. The former India spinner has said that if India did not want Rohit in the 2027 World Cup, the selectors should have told him this right after the Champions Trophy 2025.

Emma Raducanu urged to learn from Roger Federer amid worrying career slideFormer British No.1 Greg Rusedski has urged Emma Raducanu to follow Roger Federer's example by maintaining greater consistency in her training and support system as she looks to overcome her injury struggles.

Rohit Sharma's retirement may force Virat Kohli to exit: Ex-Pakistan cricketerFormer Pakistan batter Basit Ali has warned that forcing Rohit Sharma into retirement could also influence Virat Kohli's future, claiming that pressure on one senior star could have repercussions for the other.

FIFA to present NBA-styled championship rings to winners of World CupFIFA will present NBA-style championship rings to the winners of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, marking the first time the global governing body has introduced the American sporting tradition. Defending champions Argentina take on Spain in Sunday's final, with history on the line.

Andy Flower rules himself out of England role, will continue at RCBRCB head coach Andy Flower has ruled himself out of the race to become England's next Test coach. Speaking to The Telegraph, Flower said that he was happy with his current role in franchise cricket.

Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra arrested in Lanka Premier League bribery caseFormer India Under-19 World Cup winner Manjot Kalra was arrested in Sri Lanka over allegations of attempting to bribe players during the Lanka Premier League, where he is a co-owner of the Jaffna Kings franchise.

What B-schools are missing? A Messi playbook for corporate leadersFor years, Lionel Messi was judged by the one trophy that eluded him. Today, with another World Cup final on the horizon, the conversation has shifted from what he failed to win to how he transformed a team, a nation and the very idea of leadership.

2nd T20I: Live Score and Updates from BulawayoZIM vs BAN, 2nd T20I: Zimbabwe have won the toss and opted to bowl first in Bulawayo. The hosts are unchanged after winning the series opener while Bangladesh have brought in Rishad Hossain for Nasum Ahmed.

No surprises as India bank on proven names for Women's Hockey World Cup 2026India have retained the same 20-player squad for the Women's Hockey World Cup, with Salima Tete to captain the side. India have opted for a perfect blend for youth and experience as they head to the Netherlands and Belgium.

Meet Keyne Yamal: Lamine's younger brother stealing the World Cup spotlightWhile Lamine Yamal prepares to face Lionel Messi in the World Cup final, his younger brother Keyne has become Spain's breakout figure from the stands. The three-year-old's antics and obvious bond with Lamine have made him the team's unofficial mascot during the tournament.

Pakistan's Mohammad Nawaz penalised after ICC anti-doping code breachPakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz has accepted a sanction after testing positive for Carboxy-THC during the 2026 T20 World Cup, with his three-month ban set to be reduced upon completion of a treatment programme.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
